examples: The symptoms of claudication can be mimicked by many other conditions which cause pain in the legs such as arthritis and nerve problems (neuropathy).
Intermittent claudication is caused by peripheral vascular disease.
Most patients find that their claudication symptoms are worse on walking uphill.
About 10% of PAD patients have a severe form of leg pain, known as claudication.
Claudication is most often a symptom of peripheral artery disease.
